findings.summary: LT1085-ADJ regulator voltage estimated as -0.664V with inverting=true; actual output is ~13V positive; 25 opamp circuits (OPA4202 quad) detected with correct inverting configuration and gain=-0.05/...

correct.description: Each OPA4202 unit is configured as an inverting amplifier with Rf=49.9 ohm and Ri=1k ohm. Gain=-Rf/Ri=-0.0499 (reported -0.05) and gain_dB=20*log10(0.0499)=-26.04 dB (reported -26.0). The feedback capacitor (C47 etc.) is also captured. Power units (unit 5) correctly reported as 'unknown' configuration.

The main board is a hierarchical design with 5 copies of reference_circuit (4 opamps + 1 LM399 each) plus the top-level power/connector sheet. The component counts match the PCB footprint counts (287 footprints), confirming cross-file consistency.

Each of the 20 LM399 reference channels has a differential output pair (Vout+/Vout-). The analyzer correctly identifies 20 differential pairs corresponding to the 20 LM399 voltage reference channels (4 reference blocks x 5 channels per block).

The schematic has PWR_FLAG symbols placed on their own 'PWR_FLAG' net, not connected to GND. The GND net lacks a power_out driver or PWR_FLAG, so the warning is a genuine ERC issue (KiCad ERC would flag this in practice).

incorrect.description: The analyzer reports estimated_vout=-0.664, inverting=true for U21 (LT1085-ADJ). The LT1085 is a positive adjustable LDO. With R16=160 ohm (OUT to ADJ) and R15=1500 ohm (ADJ to GND), the correct Vout = 1.25*(1 + 1500/160) = 12.97V. The analyzer appears to have applied a negative/inverting formula: -Vref/ratio = -0.6/0.9036 = -0.664. The 'inverting: true' flag is also wrong — this is a standard positive LDO.

findings.summary: reference_circuit sub-sheet: 14 components, correct BOM including OPA4202 (unit 1), LM399, coupled inductor, bypass caps; Decoupling capacitors (C7, C8, C47, C52, C57) near OPA4202 and LM399 not de...

correct.description: The sub-sheet analysis is accurate: 5 resistors, 5 capacitors, 2 ICs (U22 OPA4202 unit 1 and U7 LM399), 1 inductor (L_Coupled), 1 net-tie. The LM399 is correctly identified as IC type with 4 pins. The coupled inductor (L_Coupled/EPCOS B82790 common-mode filter) is correctly typed as 'inductor'.

missed.description: The reference_circuit sub-sheet contains bypass capacitors (100n on LM399 heater supply, 100n on OPA4202 power rails) but decoupling_analysis=[] for all three schematics. This is a known limitation of flat sub-sheet analysis where power rails come in via hierarchical labels rather than explicit power symbols.

findings.summary: Board stats correct: 4-layer, 160x100mm, 287 footprints, 260 nets, fully routed, ENIG finish; 24 courtyard overlaps between Lx inductors and J1 connector are real on-layer conflicts; J2 edge_cleara...

correct.description: PCB is a 4-layer board (F.Cu, In1.Cu power plane, In2.Cu power plane, B.Cu) with verified complete routing (0 unrouted), 87 vias, 1923 track segments, and ENIG surface finish. All match design intent for a precision analog burn-in board.

J1 (DIN41612 connector) courtyard x=[161-183.85], y=[75.88-144.12] on F.Cu overlaps with all L-series inductors positioned at x≈163mm on F.Cu. The 48.1mm2 overlap area calculation is mathematically correct. These are genuine courtyard violations requiring review.
The 160mm board width does exceed JLCPCB's 100x100mm standard pricing tier, requiring a larger board tier. This is a correct observation.

The design intentionally uses per-reference-block isolated ground domains (GND_heater, GND_zener) to prevent ground-loop interference between the 20 LM399 voltage references. The analyzer correctly identifies 11 domains with no zones (isolated routing), reflecting the precision analog design intent.

incorrect.description: J2 (DIN41612_C_2x32 connector) at y=149.37mm has a courtyard extending to y=236.24mm, 76.24mm past the board edge at y=160mm. This is physically correct: DIN41612 backplane connectors are designed to hang off the board edge for mating. Flagging this as a clearance violation is a false positive for this known connector type.

findings.summary: No Gerber files in repository; gerber analysis not run (no output produced)

missed.description: The LM399_Burnin_Board repo contains no Gerber files (.gbr, .gtl, .drl etc.). No gerber output file was generated. This is expected behavior — the analyzer correctly did not produce output for a missing input.
